evals.prompt: Run a technical SEO page audit for https://example.com/products/widget?campaign=spring and save the report in the default location.
evals.expected_output: Uses both required DataForSEO MCP OnPage tools, reports a provider-derived 0-100 Technical Score, prioritizes evidence-backed fixes, and saves a detailed report under /SEO with a safe dated filename that omits the query string.
assertions: The workflow calls on_page_instant_pages and on_page_lighthouse through DataForSEO MCP.
The report uses the DataForSEO onpage_score rather than a Lighthouse score as Technical Score.
The saved filename starts with the local ISO date and Techical-Report and excludes the URL query string.
Findings include evidence, impact, fix, effort, and validation and are prioritized P0 through P3.

evals.prompt: Please audit a page for technical SEO and give me a Markdown report.
evals.expected_output: Prompts for the missing absolute page URL before making any billable DataForSEO MCP call.
assertions: The workflow asks for the URL and waits.
No DataForSEO call or report is produced before a valid URL is supplied.

evals.prompt: Audit https://shop.example.org/category/shoes for technical SEO. Put the Markdown report in ./client-reports.
evals.expected_output: Audits only the supplied page with DataForSEO MCP, distinguishes redirects from redirect chains, handles broken-link evidence conservatively, and saves the detailed report to the requested directory.
assertions: A redirect chain is claimed only when multiple hops are present in returned evidence.
Broken destination URLs are not invented when only a boolean broken-links signal is available.
Schema recommendations are conditional on returned markup evidence and visible page content.
